targets.reason: Zoplicate patches and caches Zotero.Duplicates.getSearchObject() search/temp-table behavior.
contracts: getSearchObject() builds and returns a Zotero.Search backed by a duplicates temp table for the library.
The temp table / search result is stable enough to be cached and reused across calls within a collection-tree row lifecycle.
cascade_hints: chrome/content/zotero/xpcom/duplicates.js: temp-table creation (CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE) and Zotero.Search construction.
If temp-table lifecycle moved, check collectionTreeRow.js unload/onUnload and any new cache invalidation hook.

targets.reason: Zoplicate wraps _findDuplicates() and relies on its DisjointSetForest population flow.
contracts: _findDuplicates() groups duplicate item IDs by populating a DisjointSetForest via union() calls.
The forest is the single source of duplicate grouping that Zoplicate can intercept to exclude non-duplicate pairs.
cascade_hints: chrome/content/zotero/xpcom/duplicates.js: DisjointSetForest instantiation and the union() call sites inside _findDuplicates.
If grouping moved to a different structure, the [[disjoint-set-union]] interception point must be re-mapped.

targets.reason: Duplicate dialogs, bulk merge, stats, and non-duplicate checks all consume Zotero duplicate sets by item ID.
Zoplicate intercepts union() to exclude user-marked non-duplicate pairs.
contracts: union(a, b) merges the two items' sets in the DisjointSetForest; skipping a union keeps the items in separate duplicate sets.
union() receives item IDs (or nodes) that Zoplicate can map back to pairs to suppress non-duplicate merges.

targets.reason: The cached duplicate-set type and set traversal assumptions depend on the union-find object shape.
Zoplicate patches Zotero.Item._saveData() for duplicate-refresh notifier data and child re-parenting after merges.
Zoplicate delegates all duplicate merge side effects to Zotero mergeItems().
contracts: mergeItems(items, ...) merges a set of duplicate items into a master, handling relations, attachments, and trashing of non-masters.
Calling mergeItems is sufficient for Zoplicate's bulk merge; Zoplicate does not reimplement merge side effects.
cascade_hints: chrome/content/zotero/mergeItems.mjs: exported mergeItems signature and the [[merge-items-move-relations]] helper it calls.

targets.reason: Zoplicate's retained-duplicate fallback follows Zotero merge-tracking relations created during merge.
Zoplicate inserts buttons around Zotero's duplicate merge pane and relies on item-type validation behavior.
Zoplicate mirrors Zotero duplicate-pane field-prefill semantics before calling mergeItems().
Zoplicate's merge wrapper follows Zotero's duplicate-pane merge preparation behavior.
Zotero drops duplicate temp tables from the collection-tree row unload lifecycle; Zoplicate caches duplicate searches.
contracts: On a duplicates row, getSearchObject() delegates to this.ref.getSearchObject() (Zotero.Duplicates.getSearchObject) for the row's library.
The duplicate temp table is dropped on row unload (this.onUnload), the lifecycle Zoplicate's caching compensates for.
isDuplicates() and .ref.libraryID remain the row's stable identity surface that Zoplicate reads.
cascade_hints: Zoplicate patches the INNER Zotero.Duplicates.prototype.getSearchObject (see [[duplicates-get-search-object]]), NOT CollectionTreeRow.getSearchObject -- so CollectionTreeRow-level cache changes are usually benign.

EVIDENCE (deep-verify @ dev/dcf5010): caching moved from the global Zotero.CollectionTreeCache to a per-instance cache (this._cachedSearch/_cachedResults/_cachedTempTable + new clearCache()); getSearchObject early-returns this._cachedSearch. Additive; all contracts preserved.

targets.reason: Duplicate count rendering depends on Zotero's collection row DOM structure.
Zotero duplicate-pane selection expands one selected item to its duplicate set.
contracts: In a duplicates view, selecting a single item expands the selection to that item's entire duplicate set.
The duplicate-set expansion is gated on Zotero.CollectionTreeRow.isDuplicates() and a single-row selection.
cascade_hints: CONFIRMED (deep-verify @ dev/dcf5010): the duplicate-selection logic moved OFF itemTree.jsx _handleSelectionChange (now debounce-only) into the CollectionViewItemTree subclass at chrome/content/zotero/collectionViewItemTree.jsx (~_handleSelectionChange / _handleRowMouseUpDown, lines ~1550-1567). Both contracts hold there (getSetItemsByItemID expansion + isDuplicates()&&count==1&&duplicateMouseSelection gating; only added optional chaining).

release/beta (9.0.x): logic still lives in itemTree.jsx _handleSelectionChange. Keep this anchor for those tiers. Repoint the dev anchor to collectionViewItemTree.jsx only if/when this refactor lands in a release.
